朽.origin: 木 is wood and 丂 hints at the sound; together it means wood that has rotted away.

sentence.en: This piece of wood has rotted.

杀.origin: A picture of cutting something down; it means to kill.

sentence.en: The cat wants to kill that bug.

杂.origin: 九 over 木 (tree); it means mixed or miscellaneous.

sentence.en: This matter is very complicated.

权.origin: 木 (wood) with 又 (hand) for the sound; it means power or right.

sentence.en: Everyone has rights.

杆.origin: 木 (wood) with 干 for the sound; it means a pole or stick.

sentence.en: The flagpole is tall and straight.

杈.origin: 木 (branches) and 叉 (fork) where two branches fork apart.

word.en: tree fork, crotch of a tree

sentence.en: The bird builds a nest in the tree fork.

李.origin: 木 (tree) over 子 (seed/child); it names the plum tree.

sentence.en: Plums are both sweet and sour.

杏.origin: A tree 木 with a mouth 口 below, waiting for the fruit to fall; it means apricot.

sentence.en: The tree is full of apricots.

材.origin: 木 (wood) with 才 for the sound; it means timber or material.

sentence.en: Building a house needs a lot of materials.

村.origin: 木 (trees) with 寸 for the sound; it means a village among the trees.

sentence.en: My home is in a small village.

杜.origin: 木 (tree) with 土 for the sound; it can mean to stop or shut out.

word.en: to put an end to, eradicate

sentence.en: We must put an end to waste.

杠.origin: 木 (wood) and 工 (also the sound); a wooden bar or pole.

sentence.en: He plays on the horizontal bar.

杨.origin: 木 (tree) with 昜 for the sound; it names the poplar or willow tree.

sentence.en: There are many poplar trees by the road.

杭.origin: 木 (tree) with 亢 for the sound; it appears in the city name Hangzhou.

sentence.en: The scenery in Hangzhou is beautiful.

杯.origin: 木 (once wooden) with 不 for the sound; it means a cup.

sentence.en: There is a cup on the table.

板.origin: 木 (wood) with 反 for the sound; it means a board or plank.

sentence.en: The teacher writes on the blackboard.

极.origin: 木 (wood) with 及 for the sound; it means the very top or extreme.

sentence.en: The North Pole is very cold.

构.origin: 木 (wood) with 勾 for the sound; it means to build or construct.

sentence.en: This bridge's structure is very sturdy.

枉.origin: 木 (wood) with 王 for the sound; it means crooked or in vain.

sentence.en: You have wronged me.

枕.origin: 木 (once wooden) with 冘 for the sound; it means a pillow.

sentence.en: I need a pillow to sleep.

枚.origin: 木 (tree) with 攵 for the sound; used to count small flat things.

sentence.en: I have one coin.

果.origin: Fruit drawn on top of a tree 木; it means fruit or result.

sentence.en: I like to eat fruit.

枝.origin: 木 (tree) with 支 for the sound; it means a branch.

sentence.en: The bird stands on the branch.

枣.origin: A thorny tree with dates falling off it; it means jujube or date.

sentence.en: Mom bought some red dates.

枪.origin: 木 (once wooden spear) with 仓 for the sound; now it means a gun.

sentence.en: The police officer carries a pistol.

枫.origin: 木 (tree) with 风 for the sound; it names the maple tree.

sentence.en: In autumn the maple leaves turn red.

枯.origin: An old 古 tree 木 that has dried up; it means withered.

sentence.en: The flowers have withered.

枷.origin: 木 (wood) with 加 for the sound; a wooden device locked on prisoners.

sentence.en: He broke free from the shackles.

柄.origin: 木 (wood) with 丙 for the sound; it means a handle.

sentence.en: The knife handle is made of wood.

柏.origin: 木 (tree) with 白 for the sound; it names the cypress tree.

sentence.en: There are many cypress trees on the mountain.

某.origin: 甘 (sweet) over 木 (tree); it means a certain one or someone.

sentence.en: Someone took my book.

柑.origin: 木 (tree) and 甘 (sweet, also the sound); a sweet citrus fruit.

sentence.en: Tangerines are delicious.

柒.origin: A fancy form of 七 (seven) used on checks to stop fraud.

sentence.en: The check says seven yuan.

染.origin: Water 氵, many 九, and wood 木: dipping cloth in a wooden dye bowl.

sentence.en: She dyed the cloth red.

柔.origin: 矛 (spear) over 木 (wood); wood so supple it can bend. It means soft.

sentence.en: Mom speaks very gently.

柜.origin: 木 (wood) with 巨 for the sound; it means a cabinet or cupboard.

sentence.en: The clothes are kept in the cabinet.

查.origin: 木 over 旦; it means to check, investigate, or look up.

sentence.en: The teacher checks the homework.

柯.origin: 木 (tree) with 可 for the sound; it means a branch or axe handle.

sentence.en: The big tree has many branches.

柳.origin: 木 (tree) with 卯 for the sound; it names the willow tree.

sentence.en: There is a willow tree by the river.

柴.origin: 此 for the sound over 木 (wood); it means firewood.

sentence.en: Grandpa goes up the hill to gather firewood.

标.origin: 木 (tree) with 示 part; it means a mark or sign.

sentence.en: I have a small goal.

栋.origin: 木 (wood) with 东 for the sound; it means a roof beam; also counts buildings.

sentence.en: That is a tall building.

栏.origin: 木 (wood) with 兰 for the sound; it means a fence or railing.

sentence.en: Don't lean on the railing.

栓.origin: 木 (wood) with 全 for the sound; it means a peg, plug, or bolt.

sentence.en: This bolt is very tight.

栗.origin: A tree 木 bearing hard nuts on top; it names the chestnut.

sentence.en: In winter we eat chestnuts.

校.origin: 木 (wood) with 交 for the sound; it means a school.

sentence.en: I go to school every day.

株.origin: 木 (tree) with 朱 for the sound; it means a plant stump; counts plants.

sentence.en: There is a flower in the yard.

核.origin: 木 (tree) with 亥 for the sound; it means the core or pit of a fruit.

sentence.en: There is a pit inside the peach.

根.origin: 木 (tree) with 艮 for the sound; it means a root; also counts long things.

sentence.en: The big tree's roots are deep.

格.origin: 木 (wood) with 各 for the sound; it means a square, grid, or standard.

sentence.en: The notebook has little squares.
